string 2:
The client is a grade-schooler named Kurumi. She contacted
me via e-mail.

I've only read the subject. You're here just in time to look
at the rest with me.

So the subject is... "Instructor Miura at school has
disappeared."
{next}So her teacher went missing.
{next}You mean THAT Mr. Miura?!
{end}I need to take care of something else first.

Judging from that opening, yeah, I'd assume so. But it
sounds like there's more to it.

Oh, shut up. Like you even know! This is serious, so no more
jokes.

This case sounds like a long one, so if you've got something
to do, do it now.

Finished your business? Can we read that e-mail from Kurumi
now?
{next}Okay.
{end}Not yet.

Well, come back when you've got time. I'd like to help out
Kurumi sooner than later.

Okay, I'll read the e-mail then. There's some important
stuff in here, so pay attention.

Let's see... "Hello. I go to an elementary school in the
city."

"At my school, there's a famous ghost story that everyone
knows."
"The story is called 'The Scariest Face in the World.'"

"There's a book in the library that we're not supposed to read."
"The book is all red, so it's creepy and hard to miss."

"They say if you read even one page... you won't be able to
stop reading the book."

"As soon as you read to the end... you feel something tap
your back from behind..."

"And then when you turn around, you see the scariest face in
the world."
{next}Someone was just trying to get their attention.
{next}So it's a monster?
{next}Probably just library closing time.
So you'd think... but there's more.
Can't say for sure just yet. Let's read a bit more.

The librarian?! Yeah, right! Stop interrupting and just
listen to the rest!

"This story made lots of kids too scared to go near the
library. I was scared, too."

"One day, our teacher Saburo Miura heard the story and got mad."
"He said, 'Don't stop reading because of a stupid rumor!'"

"He said he'd prove it was just talk and started
reading the book in front of us."
{next}I'm with the teacher.
{next}Did he read the book out loud?
{next}There's something suspicious going on...

Yeah, kids should use the library... Anyway, it seems the
teacher read the book aloud.
Seems that way. He started reading it in a big, loud voice.

...Yeah, I kinda thought that, too. But I also get how the
teacher felt.

"...So Mr. Miura started to read the book." "'My name is
Saburo. I'm an only child, but Saburo means third-born.'"

"As soon as he read that line, Mr. Miura's face changed." "But 
he quickly got it under control and started reading again."

"'I was sent to live with my grandmother, and I couldn't
really make any friends...'"

"'Wh-When I was seven, my grandmother bought me a soccer
ball. It b-became my friend.'"

"Mr. Miura went quiet after reading up to that point. Then
he left the room."

"He didn't come back, so as class rep I went to find him in
the faculty room."

"Mr. Miura was reading the red book intently." "When another
teacher tried to talk to him, this is what he said:"

"'Class doesn't matter. I h-have to read this book. I
must!'"

"'The main character Saburo went to live with his grandmother 
and didn't have friends...'" "'...so he played soccer alone.'"

"'He played soccer like it was all that mattered.'" "'But at his final
high school tournament, his missed a penalty kick and quit.'"

"'Then I focused on my studies. I got my teaching certification...'"
"'...and I, no, Saburo, became a teacher.'"

"It all matches, you see. This is about me! And if I don't
read to the end, I... I...!"

"Mr. Miura then ran out of the school with the book. He
never came back after that."

Kurumi says there have been sightings of Mr. Miura at
Shinjuku Park.

The witnesses say he was reading a book on a bench like a
man possessed. That's the last anybody's seen of him.
{next}Let's go check Shinjuku Park.
{next}Wanna ask around?
{next}It's a made-up story, isn't it?
Hmm... Can we hold off on that for a bit? I have an idea.

No need. Kurumi already poked around a bunch and put
everything she found in the e-mail.

...The Twilight Zo— Hey, now isn't the time! I believe
Kurumi, and I'm gonna investigate!

There's something I wanna look into, so you just hang tight
here for a bit.

I'll be back in touch if I find out anything. Take a load
off while you wait.
Okay, I'm stepping out for a while. See ya later!

Looks like this is the bench from the report. There's a red
book on it...

I think... I have to read it. If my hunch is correct, I
should be fine...

"My name is Hiroko Sagisaka. I'm trying to become a
top-level streamer."
This... isn't about Mr. Miura. Am I the main character...?

...Whoa! How many hours have passed? I have to get back... I
must get back, but...

I can't tear my eyes away from this book... I have to read
more... to the very end...

J-Just one more page... "I headed to Shinjuku Park for a
case..."
"...then started reading a red book I found on a bench..."

Seems break time is over. I tracked Hiroko's movements just
in case, but something's off.

She spent some time in Shinjuku Park, then went into the sewers 
and vanished. I recommend going to help her immediately.
This is around the spot where Hiroko's signal vanished.

I imagine Hiroko entered the In-Between here. You should
investigate when you're ready.

As suspected, I'm picking Hiroko's signal up further in.
Continue on this course.
Kehehe... Be afraid! Be more afraid. More, more!
{next}So you're the one behind all of this.
{next}Why a red book?
{next}Your face is scary.

I made the red book. If that's what you mean, then, yeah,
I'm behind it all.

Kehehe... Seems you've figured out that I'm the one who made
the book.

As if that would bother me. In fact, I take it as a
compliment.

All the humans who read the red book got scared, just as I
planned.

Seeing their faces twist in terror when they read about
themselves was most excellent.

And... once their fear was at its peak, I showed them the
scariest face ever.
{next}Yes, that might be scary.
{next}Why are you doing this?
{next}I-I'm not afraid...
Of course it is. You seem to be worth scaring, too.
To scare people. Why else?

Kehehe. Your voice is trembling. I know you're actually
afraid.

I can harvest superior energy from humans who are at the
utmost limits of terror.

All my victims have offered up their fear readily. Things
couldn't be going smoother.
So... don't get in my way. Begone, pest!
Ngh... Ouch. Probably hit my head when I fell...

Oh, you must've saved me! But you could've gotten here a
little sooner, you know.
{next}Sorry to have kept you waiting.
{next}Did you let yourself get caught?
{next}I've got a scary face to show you.
Hehe. Well, awesome work. You must've picked up on my idea.
Hehe. Bingo. I figured that would be the quickest solution.
What's with the weird face? You look ridiculous.

I realized that simply finding the missing teacher wouldn't
get to the bottom of things.
We were going to need another viewpoint to learn the truth.

That's why I decided to read the book myself. Because I was
sure you'd come looking for me.
{next}Wish you would've told me first.
{next}But you were curious, too, right?
{next}I had no idea...

Yeah, I know. Sorry 'bout that. I didn't expect to find the
book just laying there.

Well, yeah. I mean, if the mysterious book is right there,
you're gonna read it, right?

What? You didn't catch on? You came here purely by
coincidence? Guess I lucked out...

Oh, there are several unconscious people trapped here, the
others who read the book.
I'm sure Kurumi's teacher, Mr. Miura, must be one of them.

We don't want them waking up here, so let's get them
outside.
Are you okay, Mr. Miura?

Yes. Sorry for worrying you. I'm still a little dizzy, but
I'll be fine.

I didn't think I was so unwell that I'd pass out. Thank you
for your assistance.

We just happened to be passing by and heard this little girl
calling for help.

We didn't really do anything, so don't worry. More
importantly, you should go get rest.

I suppose I'll be on my way. For some reason, it feels like
I haven't been home in a while.
Thank you so much for saving Mr. Miura!
No problem. Actually, thank YOU for the amazing material.

Be careful on your way home. Don't go reading any strange
books!
Okay! Bye-bye!

Looks like Mr. Miura and all the other victims have headed
home safely.
Whew, I'm kinda beat... Think I'll call it a day.

Oh, here. I found this when I was trapped. I'm sure you'll
be able to put it to use.
